tasks.title: Foundational Project Setup and CI/CD
tasks.description: This task focuses on setting up the local development infrastructure for the CareCircle ecosystem. It includes configuring Docker Compose to manage essential backend services such as PostgreSQL and Redis, initializing the core application modules and database schema as outlined in the BRD, and ensuring that the development environment is fully operational for subsequent development tasks.
tasks.details:
<info added on 2025-07-03T15:11:57.808Z>
**Completed Actions:**

- Removed all 17 unit test files (.spec.ts) from the backend NestJS application.
- Deleted 1 end-to-end test file (app.e2e-spec.ts) and the entire /test directory.
- Removed the jest-e2e.json configuration file.
- Updated the backend package.json to eliminate test scripts and test-related dependencies (@nestjs/testing, @types/jest, @types/supertest, jest, supertest, ts-jest).
- Removed the Jest configuration section from package.json.
- Updated nest-cli.json to prevent future spec file generation (generateOptions.spec: false).
- Removed the flutter_test dependency from the mobile pubspec.yaml.
- Updated the Business Requirements Document (BRD) to include a new Development Approach & Quality Assurance section.
- Created a comprehensive Product Requirements Document (PRD) outlining an MVP-first approach without automated testing.
- Verified that the build still works correctly after test removal.
- Uninstalled test packages from node_modules.

**Quality Assurance Strategy:**

- Manual testing of all features and user flows.
- Mandatory code reviews for all changes.
- Utilization of TypeScript/Dart for compile-time safety.
- Implementation of ESLint/Dart Analyzer for code consistency.
- Production monitoring and logging.
- Gradual rollout with user feedback loops.

This change allows the team to focus entirely on feature development and rapid iteration without the overhead of maintaining test infrastructure during the MVP phase.
</info added on 2025-07-03T15:11:57.808Z>

subtasks.title: Configure Environment Variables and Secrets Management
subtasks.description: Set up environment variables and secrets management for each component to handle sensitive information securely.
subtasks.details: For each repository, create environment configuration files (e.g., '.env' for backend and frontend, and 'flutter.env' for mobile) to store sensitive information such as database credentials, API keys, and secret tokens. Utilize libraries like 'dotenv' for Node.js applications and 'flutter_dotenv' for Flutter to load these variables securely. Ensure that these files are excluded from version control by adding them to the '.gitignore' file.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm that each application can access the environment variables correctly and that sensitive information is not exposed in the codebase.

subtasks.title: Set Up Local Development Environment with Docker Compose
subtasks.description: Configure Docker Compose to manage local development services, including PostgreSQL and Redis, to streamline backend development.
subtasks.details: Create a 'docker-compose.yml' file in the 'backend' repository to define services for PostgreSQL and Redis. Configure the PostgreSQL service with appropriate environment variables for user, password, and database name. Set up the Redis service with default configurations. Ensure that data volumes are specified for both services to persist data across container restarts. Reference: [NestJS + Redis + Postgres Local Development With Docker Compose](https://www.tomray.dev/nestjs-docker-compose-postgres)
subtasks.testStrategy: Run 'docker-compose up' and verify that both PostgreSQL and Redis services start successfully. Confirm that the backend application can connect to these services using the specified environment variables.

subtasks.title: Initialize Core Application Modules and Database Schema
subtasks.description: Set up the core modules and define the initial database schema for the backend application as outlined in the Business Requirements Document (BRD).
subtasks.details:
In the 'backend' repository, create the core modules such as 'User', 'Auth', and 'HealthRecord' using the NestJS CLI. Define the database schema using an ORM like Prisma or TypeORM, creating entities and relationships as specified in the BRD. Implement initial migration scripts to set up the database tables accordingly.
<info added on 2025-07-03T14:03:49.883Z>
To integrate Prisma ORM into the 'backend' repository, follow these steps:

1. **Install Prisma CLI**: In the root directory of your NestJS project, install the Prisma CLI as a development dependency:

   ```bash
   npm install --save-dev prisma
   ```


2. **Initialize Prisma**: Initialize Prisma in your project:

   ```bash
   npx prisma init
   ```


   This command creates a `prisma` directory with a `schema.prisma` file and a `.env` file for environment variables.

3. **Configure the Database Connection**: In the `.env` file, set the `DATABASE_URL` to your database connection string. For example, for PostgreSQL:

   ```
   DATABASE_URL="postgresql://USER:PASSWORD@HOST:PORT/DATABASE"
   ```


   Replace `USER`, `PASSWORD`, `HOST`, `PORT`, and `DATABASE` with your database credentials.

4. **Define the Data Model**: In the `prisma/schema.prisma` file, define your data models based on the Business Requirements Document (BRD). For example:

   ```prisma
   model User {
     id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
     email     String   @unique
     name      String?
     posts     Post[]
   }

   model Post {
     id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
     title     String
     content   String
     author    User     @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])
     authorId  Int
   }
   ```


   This defines `User` and `Post` models with their respective fields and relationships.

5. **Generate Prisma Client and Apply Migrations**: Generate the Prisma Client and apply the initial migration to set up the database schema:

   ```bash
   npx prisma generate
   npx prisma migrate dev --name init
   ```


   The `prisma generate` command generates the Prisma Client, and `prisma migrate dev` applies the migration to your database.

6. **Create a Prisma Service**: In your NestJS application, create a `PrismaService` to manage the Prisma Client instance. This service will handle database connections and can be injected into other services or controllers.

   ```typescript
   import { Injectable, OnModuleInit, OnModuleDestroy } from '@nestjs/common';
   import { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';

   @Injectable()
   export class PrismaService extends PrismaClient implements OnModuleInit, OnModuleDestroy {
     async onModuleInit() {
       await this.$connect();
     }

     async onModuleDestroy() {
       await this.$disconnect();
     }
   }
   ```


   This service extends the Prisma Client and implements lifecycle hooks to manage the database connection.

7. **Integrate Prisma Service into Modules**: Import the `PrismaService` into your application's modules and inject it into services or controllers as needed. For example, in a `UsersService`:

   ```typescript
   import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
   import { PrismaService } from '../prisma/prisma.service';

   @Injectable()
   export class UsersService {
     constructor(private prisma: PrismaService) {}

     async findAll() {
       return this.prisma.user.findMany();
     }

     async create(data: { name: string; email: string }) {
       return this.prisma.user.create({
         data,
       });
     }
   }
   ```


   This service uses the `PrismaService` to interact with the database, providing methods to find and create users.

By following these steps, you will set up Prisma ORM in your NestJS application, providing a type-safe and developer-friendly approach to database management.
</info added on 2025-07-03T14:03:49.883Z>
<info added on 2025-07-03T14:41:03.733Z>
Successfully created core application modules using NestJS CLI:

- Generated modules with services and controllers:
  - User module (with full CRUD operations, DTOs, Prisma integration)
  - Auth module (basic structure)
  - HealthRecord module (basic structure)
  - Prescription module (basic structure)
  - CareGroup module (basic structure)
  - DailyCheckIn module (basic structure)
  - Notification module (basic structure)
  - Document module (basic structure)

- Implemented comprehensive User service with:
  - Full CRUD operations
  - Proper error handling with Prisma error types
  - User profile with related data
  - Email verification and user deactivation
  - TypeScript types and validation

- Created User DTOs with validation:
  - CreateUserDto with class-validator decorators
  - UpdateUserDto using @nestjs/mapped-types

- Implemented User controller with REST endpoints:
  - POST /users (create user)
  - GET /users/:id (get user by ID)
  - GET /users/:id/profile (get user profile with relations)
  - PATCH /users/:id (update user)
  - DELETE /users/:id (delete user)
  - PATCH /users/:id/verify-email (verify email)
  - PATCH /users/:id/deactivate (deactivate user)

- All modules are properly imported in AppModule
- PrismaModule integration completed
- Installed required dependencies: @nestjs/mapped-types, class-validator, class-transformer

Next: Need to implement Auth service and other core modules with proper dependencies and business logic.
</info added on 2025-07-03T14:41:03.733Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Run migration scripts against a development database and verify that the tables and relationships are created as expected. Implement unit tests for the core modules to ensure they function correctly.

subtasks.title: Initialize Project Repositories and Directory Structure
subtasks.description: Set up the foundational repositories and directory structure for the CareCircle ecosystem, including the NestJS backend, Next.js admin portal, and Flutter mobile app.
subtasks.details: Create three separate repositories: 'backend' for the NestJS application, 'frontend' for the Next.js admin portal, and 'mobile' for the Flutter mobile app. Within each repository, establish the standard directory structure appropriate for each framework. For the backend, include directories such as 'src', 'test', and 'config'. For the frontend, include 'pages', 'components', and 'public'. For the mobile app, include 'lib', 'test', and 'assets'.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that each repository contains the correct directory structure and that the initial commit includes all necessary files.

tasks.title: User Account & Profile Management (FR-01)
tasks.description: [Backend/NestJS, Mobile/Flutter, Frontend/Next.js] Implement user registration, login (email/password, Google/Apple SSO), profile management, and data consent flows as per FR-01.

subtasks.title: Implement User Registration and Login Endpoints with Validation
subtasks.description: Develop backend endpoints for user registration and login, incorporating input validation and password hashing.
subtasks.details:
Create RESTful endpoints in the NestJS backend for user registration and login. Utilize the 'class-validator' package to enforce input validation rules. Implement password hashing using 'bcrypt' to securely store user passwords. Ensure that the Prisma user model is updated accordingly.
<info added on 2025-07-03T15:29:46.152Z>
Implemented user registration and login endpoints with comprehensive validation and security features:

1. User registration endpoint with email and password validation using class-validator decorators.
2. Secure password hashing using bcrypt with 12 salt rounds.
3. User login endpoint with proper authentication flow.
4. Input validation for all DTOs.
5. Error handling for duplicate users and invalid credentials.
6. Integration with existing Prisma User model.

All endpoints are functional, and the build is successful.
</info added on 2025-07-03T15:29:46.152Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests to verify input validation logic and password hashing functionality. Use integration tests to confirm that the registration and login endpoints function correctly with valid and invalid inputs.

subtasks.title: Integrate JWT Authentication with Passport.js
subtasks.description: Set up JWT-based authentication using Passport.js to manage user sessions securely.
subtasks.details:
Install and configure the '@nestjs/passport' and '@nestjs/jwt' packages. Define a JWT strategy that extracts tokens from the Authorization header and validates them. Implement guards to protect routes that require authentication. Ensure that the JWT secret is securely stored and managed.
<info added on 2025-07-03T15:29:59.827Z>
Implemented a comprehensive JWT authentication system using Passport.js, including:

1. JWT Strategy with proper token validation and user lookup.
2. Local Strategy for username/password authentication.
3. JWT and Local Auth Guards for route protection.
4. Token generation with access tokens (15 minutes) and refresh tokens (7 days).
5. Refresh token endpoint for token renewal.
6. Password reset functionality with a secure token-based flow.
7. Environment configuration for JWT secrets.
8. Updated auth module with all strategies and JWT module configuration.
9. Type-safe payload interfaces for all JWT operations.

Backend build completed successfully with no errors.
</info added on 2025-07-03T15:29:59.827Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Develop unit tests for the JWT strategy to ensure correct token validation. Create integration tests to verify that protected routes are accessible only with valid tokens.

subtasks.title: Implement Google OAuth and Apple Sign-In Integration
subtasks.description: Enable users to authenticate using Google and Apple accounts through OAuth integration.
subtasks.details: For the backend, configure Passport.js strategies for Google and Apple OAuth. For the frontend, use the 'google_sign_in' and 'sign_in_with_apple' packages in Flutter to handle the authentication flows. Ensure that the backend can process the OAuth tokens received from the frontend and create or link user accounts accordingly.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests for the OAuth strategies to ensure correct handling of tokens and user information. Perform end-to-end tests to verify the complete authentication flow from frontend to backend.

subtasks.title: Develop Password Reset Functionality
subtasks.description: Create a secure mechanism for users to reset their passwords.
subtasks.details: Implement an endpoint that allows users to request a password reset, generating a secure token and sending it via email. Create another endpoint to handle the actual password reset using the token. Ensure that tokens are time-limited and securely stored.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests to verify token generation, storage, and validation. Conduct integration tests to ensure the password reset process works as intended, including email delivery and token expiration handling.

subtasks.title: Implement Session Management and Refresh Tokens
subtasks.description: Manage user sessions effectively by implementing refresh tokens for prolonged authentication.
subtasks.details: Enhance the authentication system to issue refresh tokens alongside access tokens. Store refresh tokens securely and implement an endpoint to handle token refreshing. Ensure that refresh tokens can be revoked when necessary.
subtasks.testStrategy: Develop unit tests to verify the issuance and validation of refresh tokens. Create integration tests to ensure the token refresh process works correctly and securely.

subtasks.title: Implement Data Consent and Privacy Controls
subtasks.description: Provide users with options to manage their data consent and privacy settings.
subtasks.details: Develop endpoints that allow users to view, grant, or revoke data consent. Implement mechanisms to handle user data according to their consent preferences. Ensure compliance with relevant data protection regulations.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests to verify the correct handling of consent preferences. Conduct integration tests to ensure that user data is managed appropriately based on consent settings.

tasks.title: Health Data Integration (FR-02)
tasks.description: [Mobile/Flutter, Backend/NestJS] Integrate with Apple HealthKit and Google Fit to sync, process, and display biometric and activity data. The backend will store and manage the synced data.

tasks.title: Prescription Management (OCR Scanner) (FR-03)
tasks.description: [Mobile/Flutter, Backend/NestJS] Develop the OCR-based prescription scanner, including image capture, data extraction (drug name, dosage, schedule), and manual entry/edit capabilities. The backend will handle the AI/OCR processing.

tasks.title: Intelligent Notification & Reminder System (FR-04)
tasks.description: [Backend/NestJS, Mobile/Flutter] Build the smart reminder system with actionable notifications, audit logging, and the adaptive notification engine. The mobile app will receive and display the notifications.

tasks.title: Intelligent Daily Check-ins (FR-05)
tasks.description: [Mobile/Flutter, Backend/NestJS] Implement the adaptive daily check-in feature with interactive notifications and personalized question flows. The backend will process and store the check-in data.

tasks.title: Care Groups & Family Network (FR-06)
tasks.description: [Backend/NestJS, Mobile/Flutter, Frontend/Next.js] Create the Care Group functionality, including secure invitations, deep linking, role-based permissions, and the shared dashboard.

tasks.title: AI-Powered Insights & Summaries (FR-07)
tasks.description: [Backend/NestJS] Develop the RAG-based AI system to generate summaries for medications, health conditions, and user data trends.

tasks.title: Document Export & Sharing (FR-08)
tasks.description: [Mobile/Flutter] Implement the feature to export user health summaries to a shareable PDF document.

tasks.title: Administrative Web Portal (FR-09)
tasks.description: [Frontend/Next.js] Build the Next.js admin portal with system health dashboards, user management tools, and AI cost monitoring.

tasks.title: User Settings & Personalization (FR-10)
tasks.description: [Mobile/Flutter, Frontend/Next.js, Backend/NestJS] Implement the comprehensive user settings screen, including modular UI, granular notification controls, and privacy settings.

tasks.title: Monetization & Growth Strategy (FR-11)
tasks.description: [Backend/NestJS, Mobile/Flutter] Implement the freemium model, in-app subscription purchases (IAP, MoMo, ZaloPay), and the user referral program.

tasks.title: Gamification & Engagement (FR-12)
tasks.description: [Backend/NestJS, Mobile/Flutter] Develop the Health Points system, badges, and achievements to drive user engagement.

tasks.title: Accessibility & Elder Mode (FR-13)
tasks.description: [Mobile/Flutter] Implement the simplified 'Elder Mode' with a high-contrast UI, large fonts, and voice prompts.

tasks.title: E-Pharmacy Integration (FR-15)
tasks.description: [Mobile/Flutter, Backend/NestJS] Integrate with partner e-pharmacy platforms for medication refill requests.

tasks.title: User Feedback (FR-18)
tasks.description: [Mobile/Flutter, Frontend/Next.js, Backend/NestJS] Add an in-app mechanism for users to submit feedback and bug reports.
